content.problemStatement:
Suppose f(X,Y,Z)f(X, Y, Z)f(X,Y,Z) denotes the number of arrays AAA of length XXX such that:


1≤Ai≤Y1 \leq A_i \leq Y1≤Ai​≤Y, for all (1≤i≤X)(1 \leq i \leq X)(1≤i≤X);
There are **at most** ZZZ values of iii (2≤i≤X)(2 \leq i \leq X)(2≤i≤X) such that Ai=Ai−1A_i=A_{i-1}Ai​=Ai−1​. In other words, for at most ZZZ indices, the element at a given index is equal to the previous element.
You are given an integer MMM and QQQ queries. The ithi^{th}ith query is of the form:


NiN_iNi​ KiK_iKi​: Given integers NiN_iNi​ and KiK_iKi​, find the value f(Ni,M,Ki)f(N_i,M,K_i)f(Ni​,M,Ki​).
Since output might be too large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer MMM.
The second line of input contains an integer QQQ, the number of queries.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two integers NiN_iNi​ and KiK_iKi​.


Output Format
Print QQQ lines, where, in the ithi^{th}ith line, print a single integer f(Ni,M,Ki)f(N_i,M,K_i)f(Ni​,M,Ki​), the answer for ithi^{th}ith query modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
